data.body:
Plan-review PASS (signed, 0 blocking, 10 advisory). Triage + concrete decisions:
- [E2 f2bb3b/f066be — what est_tokens counts + naming] The fit test is over the full verify REQUEST text = est_tokens(system_prompt_text + verify_instructions(chunk)); 'request' in the AC == that string. Use one name (request) in code+docs.
- [E2 f5f807 / single-oversized] 'too large' = est_tokens(request for a SINGLE finding) > budget. Escalation: models_at_or_above(resolved_verify_model); if even the top model's window can't fit it, mark that finding INDETERMINATE (non-blocking, surfaced) — never drop.
- [T2 fbc3cd] PER_FINDING_VERIFY_TOKENS default 256 is a documented, config-adjustable RESERVE (output scales per finding), not a derived constant; name it + document the basis (the plan_review_verification per-finding object size) and that it's tunable.
- [T4 f67b55] Rollout: the workflow already does ONE aggregate verify (bespoke's batch_size=12 was deleted in WS1), so this ADDS splitting only for >budget; the common case is byte-identical (one call). Back-out = the headroom knob / the splitting only triggers over budget.
- [E3 fcae03] Current verify-path callers: only the gate workflow (review_plan + drift probe via produce_plan_review_verdict) — both go through the same verify step, so chunking is single-site.
- [F1 ff3916 / E3 fb8f28] The >budget test uses a deterministic window-shrink seam (shrink largest_window_tokens in the test) so a small findings set exceeds budget → asserts principled splitting (N>1 calls, merged by index), NOT count-12; and asserts est+headroom keeps the per-chunk request under the (shrunk) window.
- [E1 fbda4a / F4 f29fb9] AC-7 (make lint/test) is a standard hygiene gate; beneficiary = an operator/agent running plan-review on a pathological huge-findings ticket (verify no longer blows the context window). Both noted, no change needed.
